def: s.m. An adversary, antagonist, an enemy; — ḵẖaṣam (used by the Persians and Indians in the sense of) master, owner; husband
more: n. m. خصم the side. 1. A master; owner; proprietor. 2. Husband ( patī ). 1. Aurat kā khasam mard, aur mard kā khasam rozgār . Prov. The husband of the wife is man, the man's husband is his livelihood. 2. Ek jorū kī jorū, ek jorū kā khasam, Ek jorū kā sīs-phūl, ek jorū kī pasham . Prov. Some wives have women wed, some men as husbands own, One's husband's so much dirt, one's of her head the crown. 3. Jorū khasam kī laṛāī kyā? Prov. It's only a quarrel between husband and wife. 4. Jorū ne mārā khasam ko, koī dauṛyo re! Song. Run, folks, run! the husband's beaten by his wife! 5. Tulsī! Rām bharose baiṭh-ke līyā pāp bhar moṭ, Joṅ bibhichārī nār ko baṛī khasam kī oṭ . Tulsī Dās. Tulsī, relying on Ram's grace you load yourself with sin, As faithless wives their living husbands certain screens find in. 6. Khasam se chhūṭe to yāroṅ ke jāë . Prov. Free from the husband, off to the lover. khasam-ro…
s. m. 1. An enemy, antagonist. 2. ¶ ḵẖaṣam, A husband. خصم والي ḵẖaṣam-wālī, adj. f. A woman whose husband is alive.
subs: ḵẖaṣam-roʼī, s.f. 'Who has wept for her husband'; a woman who has lost her husband; — (used as a malediction) may your husband die
s.f. A woman whose husband is alive.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خصم (xasm, “adversary; master, husband”), from Arabic خَصْم (ḵaṣm). Cognate with Punjabi کَھسَم (khasam), Haryanvi खसम (khasam).

def: v. a. S. कृष 1. To pull; pluck; tear (اپارَنا 1). 1. Jiskī godī meṅ baiṭhnā usī kī ḍāṛhī khasoṭnā . Prov. In his lap reared, he plucks his beard. (He cuts off the hand that feeds him). 2. Maryo yoh tāpan-hāre rī! āp tāp-kar jāḍā tāreṅ Chhān khasoṭen mhāre rī . Rus. Wom. Die, squatters by the fire! They take the straw from off my thatch to make themselves a fire. 2. To snatch; plunder. Kapṛe khasoṭ līye. khasoṭ'ā , n. m. 1. A trick in wrestling. 2. A disgraced person (an abuse). Āj se kaun khasoṭī ham se bole? Wom.

ety: v.n. fr. خصّ 'to distinguish or particularise, &c.
def: adj. Distinguished (from others), particular, peculiar, special, distinct; private; kept for private use (of a king or a master), personal, own, proper; — choice, select, choicest, best; pure, unmixed, unadulterated; excellent, noble; — adv. Particularly, peculiarly, especially, &c.
more: adj. خص particularized, appropriated. 1. Private; personal; individual Yeh khās āp hī kī ināyat hai . 2. Specific; special; appertaining to; own; proper; appropriate; peculiar; particular; distinct; definite. Āp khās Dehlī ke rahne-vāle haiṅ? 3. Best; chief; very; extraordinary. 4. Favourite; loved; elect. Khās bande. khās apīl . A special appeal. khās-bardār , n. m. An attendant carrying the arms of his lord; one armed with a firelock. khās-patī , W. n. f. Sweetmeats of various kinds, brought in as desert. khās tahsīl . Government collections without the intervention of a zamīṅdār or farmer of the revenue. khās takrār . Material point or issue; particular issue. khās chelā ; H. pradhān chelā , n. m. The chief disciple in a monastery, the destined successor of the Mahant or head of a religious establishment of ascetics or mendicants. khās haq . Prerogative; special privilege. khās khās l…

(vulg. ḵẖāṣ, from خصّ Heb. To cut, to divide, to appropriate) adj. 1. Excellent, noble, pure, unmixed. 2. Particular, private, what is kept for the kingʼs (or the masterʼs) private use, own, proper, peculiar, single, gentle. 3. adv. Especially, peculiarly. خاص بردار ḵẖāṣṣ-bardār, s. m. A servant who, in a great manʼs retinue, carries a firelock. خاص خواص ḵẖāṣṣ-ḵẖaẉāṣṣ, A royal female servant. خاصدان ḵẖāṣṣ-dān, A kind of canteen to carry water-vessels in. خاص محل ḵẖāṣṣ-maḥal, s. m. Private apartment, the apartments of married women (in opposition to ḵẖụrd-maḥal); met. First married wife. خاص وعام ḵẖāṣṣ o āﻌmm, Noble and plebeian.
subs: s.m. An attendant who carries the arms of his master; — a servant who (in a great man's retinue) carries a firelock
s.f. Sweetmeats of various kinds brought in as dessert
s.f. Government collections without the intervention of a zamīndār or farmer of the revenue
s.m. The head disciple in a monastery (the destined successor of the mahant or head of the establishment)

s.m. Land of which the revenue is collected by the Government officers immediately from the cultivators

s.m. An estate managed by the Government
Private (female) apartment, the apartments of married women (op to ḵẖurd-maḥal, 'apartments of concubines'); (met.) first married wife
s.m. A private clerk or secretary
adj. & s.m. Noble and plebeian, high and low; the public.

def: part. adj. Wounded, broken, &c.
more: past tense 3d pers. sing. (of خستن) Wounded, was wounded or broken.

ety: part. perf. of ḵẖastan; fr. Zend qīś
def: part. adj. Wounded, hurt; broken; infirm; sick, sorrowful; — fragile, brittle; crisp, short, light (as pastry)
more: adj. 1. Broken; wounded. 2. Crispy ( bhurbhurā ). Aisī khastah ki hoṭoṅ se toṛ lo . 3. ( Met .) Wounded; heart-broken; heart-sick. khastah jāṅ, jigar, yā dil , Poet. adj. Afflicted ( khastah , 3.). khastah-hāl, phaṭe-hāl , adj. Afflicted; distressed; in bad circumstances.

adj. 1. Wounded, broken. 2. Sick, sorrowful. خسته حال ḵẖasta-ḥāl, adj. Afflicted, distressed. خسته حالي ḵẖasta-ḥāl-ī, s. f. Affliction, distress. خسته جان ḵẖasta-jān or خسته جگر ḵẖasta-jigar or خسته دل ḵẖasta-dil, Heart-broken, sick at heart. خسته دلي ḵẖasta-dil-ī, Wound or disease at heart, the having the heart broken. خسته جاني ḵẖasta-jān-ī, s. f. Broken heart, dejection of mind.
subs: adj. In bad circumstances, in a bad situation; afflicted, distressed
s.f. The state of being heart-broken; wound or disease at heart.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَسْتَه (xasta). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi خستہ (xsth /⁠xasta⁠/).

ety: perf. part. of خاستن; rt. S. śvas; Zend qād?
def: part. adj. Risen, got up, sprung up (used in comp.).
more: (from خاستن) part. pass. (in compos.) Risen, got up.

def: s.f. Wound, sore; sickness; fatigue, exhaustion; — fragileness; brittleness; crispiness (as of pastry).
more: (from خسته) s. f. 1. A wound, a sore. 2. Sickness. 3. Fatigue.

ety: prob. S. क्षपितं; or क्षिप्तं; Prk. खित्तं
def: s.f. Rubbish, refuse, filth, dirt, dung, muck, manure; anything rotten or decayed
more: n. f. 1. Manure; dung; night soil. Khāt paṛe to khet, nahīṅ bhūṛ kā ret . Rus. Prov. Manure it, it's a field; else sand is all the yield. 2. Any thing rotten or decayed. 3. Small pieces of dried cowdung cakes. Uploṅ kā khāt kar dīyā! khāt ḍālnā , v. n. To manure land. khāt kūṛā , n. m. Sweepings; refuse.

n. f. Dim. khaṭolā, khaṭolnā , n. m. S. खट्वा A bedstead ( chārpāī ). 1. Khāṭ bunā lo! Street cry. 2. Khāṭ kāṭ dī . Wom. See chārpāī kāt dī . 3. Bāolī khāṭ ke bāole pāë, bāolī rāṅḍ ke bāole jāë . Prov. A crooked bed on crooked legs, a widow mad mad children had. 4. Sās kī khāt, susar ko palkā, moë jeṭh ko peṭ batāve, chaprī moë sās uḍāve . Rus. Wom. song. My mother-in-law's bed here, there sleeps my father-in-law, They say I'm pregnant by my elder brother-in-law, I'm falsely blamed for nothing by my mother-in-law. khāṭ se utār lenā , v. a. To take down a dying man from his bed to the floor. Hin. khāṭ se lag jānā , v. n. To be bedridden. khāṭ khaṭolā, badhnā boryā, angaṛ khangaṛ , n. m. Household goods ( akhtar bakhtar ). khāṭ meṅ paṛnā, khāṭ senā , v. n. See chār-pāī par paṛnā . 1. Bālā-pan to khel gaṅvāyā, taran huā nārī bas kā re, Bridh bhayā kaph, bāë ne gherā, khāṭ paṛā na jā maskā r…
s. m. 1. A pit. 2. A subterraneous granary. (In Hind. کهاو).
subs: or khāt ḍālnā (-meṅ), To dung, to manure (land)

def: s.m. An excavation; a ditch; a pit or well; a vault; a subterraneous granary (see khattā).
s.f. A bedstead; — a cot on which a corpse is conveyed to the burning-ground, a bier
subs: v.t. To take down (a dying man) from his bed
s.m. Bed and baggage, goods and chattels.

def: adj. Six
subs: (ʻsix-legged'), s.m. A species of large black bee
(ʻhaving six compartments'), s.m. A kind of rake for breaking up the soil into small beds
(ʻsix songs'), s.m. Discordant singing; jarring, discord, wrangling; — khaṭ-rāg lānā, To make a noise or row; to create discord

def: s.f. Sound of knocking or rapping, &c.; knock, rap; (in comp.) noise, clatter, racket, row; wrangling, &c.
subs: or khaṭ-ā-paṭī, s.f. Rattle, noise, sound, clatter (of a horse's hoofs, &c.), clang, clashing (of weapons); wrangling, squabbling, quarrelling, contention, strife, conflict
adj. & s.m. Noisy, rackety; contentious, quarrelsome; — a noisy fellow; a quarrelsome person, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Fomenting quarrels, mischief-making, mischievous, turbulent, seditious; — one who stirs up strife, mischief-maker, sedition-monger, &c. (syn. fitna-angez)
s.f. Repeated or continuous knocking or rapping; rat-tat; — noise, sound, clatter, hammering; wrangling, &c. ( = khaṭ-paṭ)

discord, contention, wrangling; — trouble, difficulty; entanglement, complication; hitch; care, anxiety; troublesome business or affair, task, worldly affair; expedient, artifice, art, way, means (syn. bakherā); — a rattle (to frighten away birds, &c.)
To knock, rap, rattle, make a clatter, &c. ( = khaṭkhaṭānā)
(-iyā = S. इकः; Prk. इयो), adj. & s.m. Noisy, rackety; restless, fidgety, uneasy; contentious, quarrelsome; — a noisy fellow; a wrangler, brawler, &c.

r: khaṭ khaṭ, khuṭ khuṭ, khuṛ khuṛ
def: n. f. Rat-a-tat-tat.
s.f. Bedstead, bed (used in comp.)
more: adj. Six. کهٿ پد षट्पद kh̤aṭ-pad (six-legged) s. m. See الي alī. کهٿ رس षट्रस kh̤aṭ-ras, Six tastes. See under S. رس ras, sign. 2. کهٿ شاستر षट्शास्त्र kh̤aṭ-shāstr, The six philosophical sciences of the Hindūs (called otherwise षट्दर्शन kh̤aṭ- or sh̤aṭ-darshan ) viz. 1. न्याय nyāya, Logick, taught by गोतम Gotama. 2. वैशेषिक vaishesh̤ika, taught by कणाद Kaṇāda. It agrees with न्याय nyāya in some points and differs in others. 3. मीमांसा mīmānsā, taught by जैमिनि Jaimini. It consists of rules for the construction and right interpretation of the Vedas. 4. वेदांत vedānta, taught by व्यासदेव Vyāsadeva. The science of theology. 5. सांख्य sānkhya, taught by कपिल Kapila. The followers of this sect disbelieve in a creator, saying that the universe is from all eternity. 6. पातंजल pātanjal, introduced by शेषनाग Shesh̤anāga. It agrees in every point with Sānkhya, excepting that it makes God to be th…
subs: (dialec.) khaṭ-kirwā, or khaṭ-gīr, or khaṭ-mal, s.m. A bug

def: s.f. A wrong action, fault; a mistake, an error; an unintentional fault or offence, a slip, an oversight; failure; miss (as of an arrow, &c.)
more: n. f. خطي he did wrong. 1. A slip; oversight. See bhūl and taqsīr . 1. Khatā kare bīvī, pakṛī jāë bāṅdī . Prov. The mistress sins, the slave pays for it. 2. Tulsī mūrakh māne nahīṅ jab lag khatā na khāë, Jaise bidhvā istirī garab rahe pachhtāë . Dohā. Tulsī! the fool minds not until he breaks his shin, As widows sore repent when wombs betray their sin. 3. Ek khatā, do khatā, tīsrī khatā mādar ba-khatā . Prov. One fault may pass, two faults may pass, the third is of the mother born. 2. A miss; failure. Hukm-andāz kā tīr khatā nahīṅ kartā. khatā k. , v. a. 1. To miss; fail. 2. To err; commit a fault ( bhūlnā ). khatā-vār , adj. Faulty; guilty. See taqsīr-vār. khatā h. , v. n. 1. To fail; err. 2. To pass involuntarily fœces or urine. Peshāb khatā ho gayā .

(from خطي Heb. To err) s. f. A mistake, errour, fault, crime. خطا بخش ḵẖat̤ā-baḵẖsh, A forgiver of errours, &c. خطا کرنا ḵẖat̤ā karnā, To err, miss, blunder. ¶ ḵẖat̤ā, n. prop. A part of Tartary, or northern China.
subs: s.m. A forgiver of faults or errors, &c.
To commit a fault, to err, to blunder, to be wrong or at fault; to miss, to fail (of)
v.n. An error to be committed (by, -se); — to fail, be deficient; — to pass involuntarily (as urine, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَطَا (xatā), from Arabic خَطَأ (ḵaṭaʔ). First attested in c. 1578 as Middle Hindi خطا (xt̤a /⁠xatā⁠/).

def: prop. n. The country of Scythia beyond Imaus, Northern China (the Cathay of Marco Polo and other travellers).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَطَا (xatā), from Arabic خَطَأ (ḵaṭaʔ). First attested in c. 1578 as Middle Hindi خطا (xt̤a /⁠xatā⁠/).

def: s.m. = khāt or khattā, qq.v.
more: n. m. 1. An account; a ledger. 2. A merchant's or banker's book ( bahī , 2). khātā ḍālnā , v. a. To open accounts with. khātāk. , v. a. To post an account; adjust accounts. chittiyā khātā . Absurdity in accounts or dealings; folly. gāl khātā , n. m. Irrecoverable balance; doubtful recoveries. khāte bāqī . Balance of account. khāte paṛnā , v. n. To be entered in an account.
s. m. 1. The act of eating. 2. Daily account, waste-book. کهاتا باڙي khātā-bāṛī, s. f. A storehouse.
subs: s.f. A subterraneous granary, a store-house.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit [Term?], from Sanskrit क्षत्र (kṣatrá, “rule, authority”).

ety: imperf. part. of khānā; and = Prk. खादंतओ, खाअंतओ; S. खादत्+अ+कः
def: part. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Eating; consuming; — expensive, costly; — who or what eats or consumes, &c.; eater; — the act of eating, &c.
subs: adj. 'Eating and drinking'; in easy circumstances, well-off, flourishing
To be ungrateful in the midst of abundance; to quarrel with one's bread and butter.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit [Term?], from Sanskrit क्षत्र (kṣatrá, “rule, authority”).

ety: prob. S. क्षत+कः, or i.q. khātā, 'eating'
def: s.m. Ledger or day-book (of a merchant or banker, &c.), account-book, waste-book; daily account, account-current
subs: s.f. An abstract of individual accounts
s.f. The book framed from the day-book, containing the distinct accounts of individuals
(-ko), To enter or post (an account); to adjust (accounts)
v.n. To be entered in an account or in a ledger
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit [Term?], from Sanskrit क्षत्र (kṣatrá, “rule, authority”).

ety: for A. خطابة, inf. n. of خطب 'to recite'
def: s.f. Preaching; eloquence, rhetoric.
direct plural of خَطا (xatā, “mistake; error”)
s. f. (dakh.) Food, eating: see خوراک ḵẖụrāk.
s.f. Making fast o firm: stopping; — a remaining still or firm(?).

ety: khaṭṭā+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Sourness, acidity, tartness ( = khaṭās, khaṭṭāpan); — an acid, souring matter (as used in culinary or chemical preparations)
subs: s.m. One who is in the habit of taking acids; (met.) an impotent man
To be put into an acid; — to be put or laid aside
(-ko), To put into an acid; to lay aside; (fig.) to keep in a state of suspense; to shilly-shally with.
wety: From Prakrit अइआ, from Sanskrit ता (tā) + इका (-ikā).

def: n. f. 1. ( khaṭṭā-pan, khaṭās ) Acidity ( turshī , 1.). 2. An acid. Gayā mard jin khāī khaṭāī, gāī rāṅḍ jin khāī miṭhāī . Prov. Much acids make men impotent, while sweets Send women from their houses on the streets. khaṭāī-khānā , n. m. 1. One addicted to acids. 2. ( Met .) An impotent. khaṭāī meṅ paṛnā , v. n. 1. To be put in an acid. 2. To be laid on the shelf. 1. Bāt khaṭāī meṅ paṛ gaī . 2. Kā hammar rupaīyā khaṭāī meṅ paṛ gel ? Mag. khaṭāī meṅ ḍālnā , v. a. 1. To put in an acid. Ghanṭe bhar meṅ lenā, khaṭāī meṅ ḍāl rakkhā hai . Goldsmiths. 2. To dilly dally ( āj kal k .). Javāb-i-sāf de do, fāëdah kyā tursh-rūī se, Hameṅ kyoṅ ḍāl rakkhā hai, ajī, tum ne khaṭāī meṅ. khaṭṭe mīṭhe din , Evil days. khaṭṭe mīṭhe ko jī chalnā , v. n. To desire sweet and sour things, esp. during pregnancy.
s. f. 1. Acidity, sourness. 2. An acid.
more: s. f. Perfidy. See کهوٿاٴي khoṭā-ī.

def: adj. Of or belonging to ḵẖat̤ā or Cathay; manufactured in Cathay.
more: adj. Of Khat̤ā or northern China.
wety: خَطا (xatā, “Cathay, northern China”) + ـی (-ī)

def: s.m. = khādak, q.v. s.m. (f. -ikā), = S کهادي खादी khādī, s.m. (f. -inī), An eater; one who borrows or uses, borrower; user; debtor.

ety: khaṭ, q.v.+āk = Prk. आवको = S. आपकः; cf. S. उड्डापकः
def: s.m. A crash ( = khaṛākā)

s.f. = khaṭkā, q.v. s.m. Knocking, rapping, rattling (of a stick, &c.); knock, rap, rattling sound or noise, clatter; sound of footsteps; — a rattle, clapper (to scare away birds = khaṭkhaṭā); a bolt, catch; — a pricking or rankling (in the mind); offence; a catch or hitch (in the mind), scruple, demur, misgiving, suspicion, doubt, suspense; perturbation, anxiety, care, concern; apprehension, fear, dread; — loss (in trade, &c.; in this sense generally khuṭkā); — a shake, or trill (in music, or in a voice)
subs: (-ko), To have a misgiving; to feel apprehension, to be apprehensive, to fear, &c.
(-kā), To listen for the sound of footsteps
adv. Without scruple or misgiving; without apprehension or fear; unhesitatingly; smoothly, easily.

ety: khaṭ, q.v.+āk = Prk. आवको = S. आपकः; cf. S. उड्डापकः
def: s.m. A crash ( = khaṛākā)
n. m. A crash. 'Lali! jau, khaṭākā kahāṅ bhao?' 'Kakyā, kaṛhī kī kaṭhautī girī .' Farrukh. Wom. Child! what crash was that? Aunt! the cover of the curry dish came down.

ety: khuṭak˚ = Prk. खुट्टक्के(इ), fr. S. खुड ( = कण्ड?)+कृ
def: v.t. To peck; to nibble; to break by pecking, &c. (as a chicken, the shell of an egg when the time for its being hatched is complete, e.g. anḍā khuṭaknā).

ety: khaṭak˚, prob. fr. S. खट+कृ; cf. kharaknā
def: v.n. To sound; to rattle; — to wrangle, quarrel; — to beat, go pit-a-pat; to feel doubt or misgiving; to excite doubt or misgiving (in, -meṅ); to feel hesitation, to hesitate, to waver; to feel anxiety or apprehension; — to prick, to rankle (in, -meṅ), to fester (in); to prove offensive (to, -ko, dat.), to be an eye-sore (to), to offend; to be averse (to or from, -se), to avoid, shun, break (with), cut (in these senses generally khuṭak-jānā); — to be impressed or struck (with, -se); — s.m. Rattling, rapping; wrangling; rankling; prick; doubt, hesitation; aversion, disgust, &c.
more: 1. v. n. To rankle, to pierce (as a thorn), offend, wrangle, doubt. 2. (sub.) Doubt, hesitation, disgust. ¶ khuṭaknā, v. a. 1. To nibble. 2. To doubt.

def: v. n. 1. To prick; rankle; to be an eyesore. 1. Āṅkh khaṭak rahī hai . 2. Ek hamārā hī dam tumhen khaṭaktā hai . Me only your eyes cant bear. 3. Do talāo, aur kitnī tiryāṅ, jab dekho tab naṅgī khaṛiyāṅ, Tāl ke ūpar din bhar bhaṭkeṅ, nazroṅ meṅ woh sab ke khaṭkeṅ . Riddle. Ans. Aṅkheṅ . Eyes. Two tanks, of women heaps, look when you like they naked stand, Just o'er the tank all day they flit, all eyes they prick like sand. 2. To cut; cut dead ( chaṭaknā , 8). Āj ham se unkī khaṭak gaī . 3. To be impressed or struck with. khaṭakte rahnā, khaṭaknā , v. a. To shun one ( alag rahnā , 1, 2).
more: v. a. 1. To peck. Bīj, kuṭaknā . 2. To come out of the egg (a chicken). Anḍā khuṭaknā .

def: n. m. Springtide. (?).
s.m. Spring-tide.

ety: prob. contrac. of khatiyān, denom. fr. khātā
def: s.f. Balancing the accounts of the day-book, &c. (cf. next).
s.m. Any near relation on the side of the wife (such as her father, or mother, or brother, &c.); — a daughter's husbaud, son-in-law.
more: s. m. 1. Circumcising. 2. A circumcision-feast. 3. (Heb. ) The nearest relations to a woman. 4. A son-in-law.

ety: caus. of khaṭnā; — prob. खटा = Prk. खंडाव˚ = S. खण्ड+आपय˚, rt. खण्ड्
def: v.t. To interrupt, obstruct; to stop, bring to a stand; to cause to tarry or stay; to make firm.
oblique plural of خَطا (xatā, “mistake; error”)
s.m. A stake or pole to which a boat is fastened (cf. khaṭānā).
more: s. m. A pin with which a boat is fastened.

def: n. m. A peg to which a boat is fastened. Dekhā ho māṅjhī! khaṭāo ukhaṛ gail! E.
s.f. See s.v. khaṭ, 'knocking.' s.f. Rubbish, refuse, filth, dirt, dung, muck, manure; anything rotten or decayed
subs: or khāt ḍālnā (-meṅ), To dung, to manure (land)

def: n. f. 1. Clashing of weapons. 2. Wrangling; quarrelling. He beṭā! āpus meṅ nit kī khaṭā paṭī nīkī nāhīṅ hott hai . khaṭā paṭī h. , v. n. To wrangle; quarrel.

ety: inf. n. of خطر 'to occur to the mind'
def: s.m. Occurring to, or coming into, the mind; thought; remembrance, recollection; — imminent danger, peril, jeopardy, risk, hazard; fortune
more: s. m. 1. Recollection, coming into the mind, thought, remembrance. 2. Danger, risk, fortune. خطرناک ḵẖat̤ar-nāk, adj. Frightful, dangerous, hazardous. خطرناکي ḵẖat̤ar-nāk-ī, s. f. Dangerousness. (Plur. خطرات).

def: s.m. A kind of insect (perhaps 'a bug,' fr. khāṭ, 'bedstead').
more: s. m. Name of an insect.

def: dangerous
wety: From Classical Persian خطرناک (xatarnāk).

ety: khaṭā = khaṭṭā+ās = wāṅs = Prk. वंछा = S. वाञ्छा
def: s.f. Sourness, acidity ( = khaṭṭāpan, khaṭāʼī).
s.f. An account of the total village lands with particulars of their distribution, &c. (see khatāwanī).
s.f. = khatāwanī, q.v.

ety: prob. fem. of khatāʼunā, denom. of khātā
def: s.f. Ledger, account-book; the roll, volume, or paper on which are abstracted into distinct heads the several items first entered in a day-book (or a field-book); — an account made up from the khasra or field-book, a classified list of cultivated holdings prepared at the time of a village survey (in which the fields belonging to each thok, paṭṭī, and individual are brought together, and the name of the occupant, the number of each field, its extent, with deductions for waste or the like, the rate of assessment per bīghā, the total rent, and the manner of its payment, are recorded).

ety: prob. for acc. of A. ḵẖat̤ā, 'fault'
def: s.f. (Arith.), Position, or the rule of false
more: n. f. ( Arith .) Position or the rule of false. khatāyan ek adad-i-farzī ; S. एक अंक कल्पिन इछ गणित Single position. khatāyan do adad-i-fārzī ; S. दो अंक कल्पित इछ गणित ( Arith .) Double position.

def: s.m. Asking or demanding a woman in marriage; contracting a marriage; marrying, betrothing; — a thing, affair, business, work, cause.
more: s. m. 1. Marrying, betrothing, contracting a marriage. 2. A thing, cause, work.

def: n. m. A bed-weaver.
direct plural; oblique/vocative singular
s.m. See s.v. khaṭ, 'bedstead.' s.f. Rubbish, refuse, filth, dirt, dung, muck, manure; anything rotten or decayed
subs: or khāt ḍālnā (-meṅ), To dung, to manure (land)

def: s.m. A caste of Hindūs who are generally employed as cart wrights; a man of that caste, a carpenter, a cartwright.
more: n. m. S. तक्षा A carpenter ( baṛhaī , 1).
s. f. Name of a cast, generally employed as cartwrights.

def: s.m. = ḵẖat̤īb, q.v. s.f. Rubbish, refuse, filth, dirt, dung, muck, manure; anything rotten or decayed
subs: or khāt ḍālnā (-meṅ), To dung, to manure (land)

def: s.m. A public speaker, an orator, a preacher, a reciter of a ḵẖut̤ba, q.v.
more: (from خطاب) s. m. A preacher, a publick speaker or orator.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خطیب (xat̤īb), itself from Arabic خَطِيب (ḵaṭīb).

def: s.f. The duty or office of a ḵẖat̤īb or preacher, &c.
more: s. f. The duty or performance of خطيب ḵẖat̤īb, q. v.

ety: act. part. n. of خطف 'to seize,' &c.
def: part. adj. Seizing, snatching, carrying off, taking away, removing.
more: part. act. (of خطف Heb. ) Snatching away (the sight).

def: s.m. A hunter, &c. (see khaṭṭik); — a low caste who live principally by keeping pigs and poultry; a man of that caste; — a tanner.
more: s. m. A hunter. See کهٿک khaṭṭik.

def: n. m. 1. A tanner. 2. A tribe who live principally by keeping pigs and poultry.
s.f. Chalk (see khaṛī).

ety: act. part. n. of ختم 'to seal'
def: s.m. The seal, or the last, of the prophets or apostles, the prophet Mohammad
more: (from ختم Heb. To seal, to close, to finish) s. f. 1. A ring worn on the fingers. 2. A seal. خاتم المرسلين ḵẖātimu-l-mursalīn or خاتم الانبيا ḵẖātimu-l-ambiyā, The seal of the prophets, i. e. Muhammad. خاتم بند ḵẖātim-band, A seal-setter.
subs: s.m. A setter of stones in rings; a seal-setter.

ety: for A. خاتمة ḵẖātimat, fem.; see ḵẖātim
def: s.m. End, issue; conclusion, finish, finis; epilogue; appendix
more: n. m. ختم stamped, sealed. 1. End; finis ; epilogue; conclusion ( ākhir , 3.). 2. Death. Khātimah bil-khaīr! A happy ending! khātime tak pahuṅchānā , v. n. 1. To bring to a termination; carry to a conclusion. 2. To put an end to; close (a business).
(from ختم) s. m. Conclusion, epilogue, finis, end. خاتمه بندي ḵẖātima-bandī, s. f. 1. A kind of workmanship of which they make bows. 2. The ceiling of a roof.
subs: s.m. A happy end or issue
s.f. A kind of workmanship in making bows; — the ceiling of a roof
v.t. To bring to an end, carry to a conclusion; to put an end to, to close (a business, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَاتِمَه (xātima), borrowed from Arabic خَاتِمَة (ḵātima).

def: adj. (dakh.) See کٿهن kaṭhin.
adj. Characterized by rank or station, eminent, noble, honourable, dignified, great, important.
more: (from خطر) adj. Great, important, honourable, dignified.
wety: From Arabic خَطِير (ḵaṭīr).

ety: part. n. of خطر 'occur to the mind,' &c.
def: s.f. Displeasure
more: n. f. خطر 1. The heart; mind. 2. Will; inclination; choice. 3. Affection; regard; respect; esteem. 4. Countenance; favour. 5. Behalf; sake. Jab bāp ke khātir rote the ab apnī khātir ro, bābā ! Nazir. 6. Plea; ground; reason. Dugānā, tū zanākhī par merī martī hai kis khātir? Bane, bī, dost jānī kaun apne jī ke dushman kā? Wom. Rangīn. Why, sister, are you dying for my bedfellow, Who ever made a friend of her life's bitter foe? 7. Pleasure; gratification; satisfaction. khātir-jamā , n. f. Assurance; satisfaction; composure; confidence. khātir-khuāh , adv. To one's satisfaction; at pleasure; to one's heart's content. Khatir-khuāh mazūrī milegī . khātir-dārī , n. f. Entertainment; warm reception; hospitality. khātir-dārī k., tavāzo k. , v. a. To pay attention to; receive or entertain hospitably, or with marked respect or regard. khātir k. , v. a. 1. To please; indulge; pay attention to. 2. T…

Melancholy, dejected, sad. خاطر آشفته ḵẖāt̤ir-āshufta, adj. Distressed, dispirited, agitated. خاطر بسته ḵẖātir-basta, Distressed in mind, oppressed in spirit. خاطر پريشان ḵẖāṭir-pareshān, adj. Troubled in mind. خاطر جمع ḵẖāt̤ir-jamﻌ, 1. adj. Collected, comforted, contented, confident, tranquil, at ease. 2. s. f. Comfort, confidence, ease of mind. خاطر خواه ḵẖāt̤ir-ḵẖẉāh, Agreeable to oneʼs wishes, satisfactory. خاطر دار ḵẖāt̤ir-dār, adj. Encouraging; comforter, encourager. خاطر داري ḵẖāt̤ir-dār-ī, s. f. Encouragement, comfort, gratification, satisfaction. خاطر داري کرني ḵẖāt̤ir-dār-ī-karnī, To comfort, to encour…

adj. & s.f. Impressed on the mind, imprinted on the memory; by heart; — impression on the mind; any mark of remembrance, &c.

adj. Fixed in the mind or memory; by heart; — chosen, beloved

ety: khāṭ, q.v.+iyā = Prk. इया, इआ; S. aff. इका
def: s.f. A small bedstead; a bier
more: s. f. 1. A bedstead. 2. A bier. خداکري اسکي کهٿيا نکلي ḵẖudā kare uskī khaṭiyā nikle, God grant his bier may be carried out, or he may die (a common form of cursing).
subs: God grant (or bring it to pass that) his bier may be carried out, or God grant be may die (a common form of cursing).

def: s.f. Account-book ( = khātā, khataunī); — entry in a ledger.

ety: khatiy˚ = khātī, fem. of khātā+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā
def: v.t. To enter in an account-book or ledger.
more: v. a. To enter in the ledger. khatau'nī ; Bhoj. khatiavnī , n. f. An account of the total village lands, with particulars of their distribution.
v. a. 1. To credit; trust ( patyānā ). Unheṅ kauṛī ko koī nahīṅ khaṭiyātā . 2. To live as friends or on good terms.

ety: khaṭiy˚ = khāṭī, fr. khāṭ, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. augment
def: v.t. To trust, to give credit to; — to have amicable dealings with, to live on friendly terms with.
s.m. Concord, harmony, amicability, good terms.
n. m. Amicability; good terms. khaṭiyāo h. , v. n. To be amicable; live in concord. Unkā yahāṅ khaṭiyāo mushkil se hogā .

n. m. 1. A knock; tap; tick; thud. 2. ( khaṭkhaṭā ) An arrangement for producing a rattling sound to scare away birds. Anāroṅ meṅ khaṭkā bāṅdh do! 3. A bolt. 4. Fear ( bhaë ). 1. Gul ko hai ek khār kā khaṭkā, bulbuloṅ ko hazār kā khaṭkā . The rose the thorn but has to fear, the nightingale amidst a thousand snares must steer. 5. Anxiety; care; concern. 2. Tere na āne kā sārī rāt rahā dil par khaṭkā . 6. Suspense ( dubdhā , 1). 7. A good voice. Iskī āvāz meṅ khaṭkā hai . 8. ( Jaipur ) A privy. khaṭkā lagnā , v. n. To fear; apprehend. be-khaṭke , adv. 1. Without fear or apprehension. 2. Smoothly; easily.
more: n. m. 1. Sound of footsteps ( āhaṭ ). 1. Ari misrānī! zarā khuṭkā letī rahiyo! Good woman! just keep your ears open, see nobody gets in. 2. Sote the jo ham, is meṅ sune gair ke khaṭke; Chhup chhup gaë uṭh donoṅ vahīṅ nīche palaṅg ke, Ham haṅste rahe, us ne ḍhabak ḍhoë jo māre, Kitnā hī taṭolā, jo ujālā ho to pāve, Chorī kī bhī rakh letī hai kyā bāt/?/ aṅdherī! Nazīr. But while in bed we lay some footsteps came that way, Under the bed we quickly crept and quiet lay. We laughed as they kept beating o'er and o'er the bed. They felt all o'er, but could they find? no light them led, Even thefts are hid—what services to darkness owed! 3. 'Haule haule ṭaṭyā khol, more deorā! Khaṛkā na sune torā bhāī re:' Man lāgā devar se, kyā jālam bhaujāī re! Bhoj. Wom. song. The matted door now gently open my young brother-in-law lest your brother hear! My heart on my young brother-in-law is set, such a tyra…

ety: khatak; fr. khaṭaknā+Prk. अओ = S. कः
def: s.m. Knocking, rapping, rattling (of a stick, &c.); knock, rap, rattling sound or noise, clatter; sound of footsteps; — a rattle, clapper (to scare away birds = khaṭkhaṭā); a bolt, catch; — a pricking or rankling (in the mind); offence; a catch or hitch (in the mind), scruple, demur, misgiving, suspicion, doubt, suspense; perturbation, anxiety, care, concern; apprehension, fear, dread; — loss (in trade, &c.; in this sense generally khuṭkā); — a shake, or trill (in music, or in a voice)
more: s. m. 1. Apprehension, suspicion, doubt, suspense, abodement. 2. Sound of footsteps.
subs: (-ko), To have a misgiving; to feel apprehension, to be apprehensive, to fear, &c.
(-kā), To listen for the sound of footsteps
adv. Without scruple or misgiving; without apprehension or fear; unhesitatingly; smoothly, easily.

def: v.t. To knock or rap at, to thump; hammer; to slam (a door, &c.); to rattle (a door, or chain, or a stick, &c.); to shoot or draw (the bolt or catch of a door); — s.m. Knocking or rapping at, rattling, &c.
more: s. m. Rapping, thumping, rattling (of a door, &c.).

def: s. f. (dakh.) 1. Noise, sound. 2. See محنت miḥnat.
s.f. by redupl. of khaṭ, q.v.
n. m. See بکهيرَا 1, 2, 3, 9, 12, and khaṭkhaṭā , 2.
more: v. n. To knock at a door; tap; rap; rattle.

ety: see khaṭ, and khaṭ-khaṭ; and cf. S. खटखटाय
def: v.t. To knock at (a door), to rap at or on, to tap, to rattle, to make a clatter with, to shake ( = khaṛkhaṛānā).
v. n. To knock, to tap, to pat, to rap.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. khaṭ, 'knock.' s.f. Rubbish, refuse, filth, dirt, dung, muck, manure; anything rotten or decayed
subs: or khāt ḍālnā (-meṅ), To dung, to manure (land)

ety: prob. khaṭ, q.v.+Prk. अडओ = S. अ+ल+कः
def: s.m. Breach, disagreement, dissension, contention; — strife; — encumbrance, impediment; possessions, belongings; wife and family, household, dependents, retinue, train; travelling domestics and things, baggage; furniture, household goods; — pile, heap; group, multitude, company; — labour, exertion; trade, business, employment; a troublesome (or intricate) business or affair; a knotty point; an affair, a matter, a case or suit.
s.m. Seal; end, conclusion, termination; a recitation or perusal of the Qǒrʼān ( = A. ḵẖatma); the feast celebrated at the end of such perusal; — adj. Done, concluded, completed, terminated
more: n. m. 1. Conclusion; end. 2. Perusal of the Qurān from the beginning to the end; the feast celebrated at its conclusion. khatm k. , v. a. To close; finish; conclude; complete. khatm h. , v. n. To be concluded. Dānā pānī khatm ho gayā . He has done with meat and drink (with this life).
s. m. 1. Conclusion, seal, end. 2. (used adjectively) Done, finished. ختم المرسلين ḵẖatmu-l-mursalīn, Seal of the sent (of God). ختم کرنا ḵẖatm karnā, To conclude, finish, complete.
subs: s.m. The seal of those sent (of God), the prophet Mohammad
v.t. To conclude, close, finish, complete, terminate (at, -par)
(-par), To be concluded, be terminated (at), be finished.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ختم (xatm), itself from Arabic خَتْم (ḵatm).

def: full stop, period ۔
wety: From ختم (xatm, “finish”) + ہ (-ah, “adjective suffix”).

def: s.m. A bug
subs: s.m. A flying bug, Ӕthus maurus (cf. koṭhmīr-kā kīṛā)
s.m. The scarlet flying bug, Dystercus cingulatus.
wety: کھٹ (“bed, cot”) + مل (“filth”).

def: n. m. A bug. Dekho re! khaṭmal kī bātāṅ, kāṭ khāë nahīṅ āve hātān . What bugs! they bite and wont be caught. uṛtā khaṭmal ; Sant. chāyā , n. m. A flying bug; Ӕthus maurus. barchhīlā pīṭh kā khaṭmal , n. m. The spear-back bug; Tetrodo bilineata. bhūrā brikshī khaṭmal , n. m. The brown tree bug; Cantao ocellatus. pan-khaṭmal , n. m. The water bug; Belostoma indica. chhachhūṅdar khaṭmal , n. m. The mole cricket; Schizo-dactylus monstrosus. sāṅpan-chhippī khaṭmal , The snake lizard. kekṛā-makṛī khaṭmal , n. m. The crab-spider or Mygale. lāl khaṭmal , n. m. The scarlet flying-bug; Dystercus cingulatus .
s.f. A recitation or perusal of the whole Qǒrʼān ( = ḵẖatm).
s.f. Marsh-mallow, Althœa officinalis.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. khaṭṭā. s.m. A pit (for storing grain, or ice, &c.), a subterraneous granary ( = khāt, and khātā); — store, heap, stock.
more: s. m. A mixed taste of sour and sweet; agreeable.

def: s. m. See کهٿميٿها.
s.m. Circumcising; circumcision; — a circumcision-feast.

ety: khaṭ˚ = Prk. खंडे(इ) = S. खण्ड ˠ(ते), pass. of rt. खण्ड
def: v.n. To be interrupted, or obstructed (a person, or a work or business); to come to a stand, to stop, to stick; to hold back doggedly or firmly, to remain firm or obstinate (in); — to put up (at a place), to stay, tarry, remain; to continue, last; to remain over, to be gained.
v. n. To gain (بتَنا 2). Rupaë ke saude meṅ do āne khaṭṭe .
To circumcise.
s.m. Circumcision ( = ḵẖatn, q.v.)

ety: khāṭ, q.v.+Prk. अउल्लओ = S. उल+कः; — or olā is a dim. affix
def: s.m. A small bedstead or bed; a rickety or dilapidated bedstead.
n. m. Dim. khaṭolī , n. f. A small bed larger than the khaṭolī .
s.f. dim. of khaṭolā, q.v.

more: s. (dakh.) 1. A bedstead. 2. (i. q. ايک قسم کا دام) A sort of net or snare (?).

def: s. f. 1. Wrangling, contention, quarrel: see غٿ پٿ g̠ẖaṭ-paṭ. 2. Clashing of weapons.

adj. & s.m. = H کهٿپٿ खटपट khaṭ-paṭ, s.f. See s.v. khaṭ, 'knock.'
s.m. = H کهٿراگ खटराग khaṭ-rāg, s.m. See s.v. khaṭ, 'six.'
s.m. = next, q.v.

ety: for A. خطرة ḵẖat̤rat, fem.; n. of un. fr. خطر
def: s.m. Danger, &c. ( = ḵẖat̤ar, q.v.); fear, apprehension
more: n. m. خطر was in peril. Danger ( andeshā , 3.). khatrah rafٴa k. , 1. To ward off danger. 2. To ease nature. khatra-i-zarar . Risk of harm. khatra-i-ٴam . A common danger. khatre meṅ ḍāl'nā , v. a. To endanger; risk; imperil; expose to danger; hazard. khatar-nāk , adj. Dangerous; perilous; hazardous.

s. m. 1. Danger. 2. Fear, risk, venture. خطره رفع کرنا ḵẖat̤ra rafﻌ karnā, To ease nature. خطره نه کرنا ḵẖat̤ra na karnā, To make no bones of, to make no difficulty; not to fear or anticipate danger. خطريمين ڐالنا ḵẖat̤re meṅ ḍalnā, To endanger, risk, venture, expose to danger, hazard.
subs: (-se), Not to fear or apprehend danger (from), not to be afraid (of); to make no bones (of), to make no difficulty
To expose to danger, &c., to endanger, imperil, to risk, hazard, venture.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَطْرَه (xatra), borrowed from Arabic خَطَر (ḵaṭar).

def: n. m. S. षट six. lit . six rāgs . 1. Singing discordantly. 2. Wrangling ( bakheṛā , 1, 3, 7, 10). Nūn tel ke khatrāg to lage rahī! Bhoj. For salt and oil in constant broil. khaṭ-rāg lānā , v. a. See rog lānā .
s. m. 1. Wrangling. 2. Singing discordantly.
s.m. = H کهٿپورا खटपूरा khaṭ-pūrā, s.m. See s.v. khaṭ, 'six.'
s.m. A young Khatrī, son of a Khatrī.
adj. Belonging to Khattrīs. khatraiṭā , n. m. (Contemp.) A son of a Khattrī .
s.f. A woman of the Kshatrī or military caste of Hindūs; — the wife of a Kshatrī.
more: s. f. The female of کهتري khatrī, q. v.

oblique plural of خَطْرہ (“risk, danger”)
vocative plural of خَطْرہ (“danger; risk”)

ety: v.n. fr. خطّ 'to make a line or mark'; 'to write'
def: s.m. A line, a streak, or stripe, a mark; lineament; — writing, character, handwriting chirography; a letter, epistle; — down on the face, incipient beard, &c.; beard; moustaches
more: n. m. خط he made a mark. 1. Handwriting; writing. 2. A letter; note; epistle. 1. Paise ko khat, āne ko chiṭṭhī . One pice for writing a Persian letter, one anna for an English letter. 2. Khat ādhī mulaqāt hai . Prov. Correspondence is half acquaintance (or meeting). 3. A note of hand. Kuchh tumhārā khat ātā hai? 4. Rumple; crease ( shikan ); scar; mark. Itnī talvāreṅ mārīṅ, magar khat tak nahīṅ ukhṛā . 5. Moustache; whiskers; beard; down on the face. Khat ne terā husn sab gaṅvāyā . 6. ( Math .) A line (H. lakīr ). khat ānā, yā nikal'nā , v. n. To appear (the first growth of hair on the face). Sāf the jab tak jāvāb-i-sāf thā, Ab jo khat * āne lagā, shāyad ke khat † āne lagā . (Beardless youth are much prized by Sodomites). No hair upon his cheek, he would refuse quite straight, With hair come out now letters from him on you wait. khatt-i-istivā ; S. मध्यरेखा, n. m. The equator; the prime …
n. m. A plot of land; region; country.

(Ch. To dig, to engrave) s. m. 1. A letter, epistle. 2. A line, lineament. 3. Writing, handwriting, chirography. 4. Mustaches, beard, youthful beard or down on the face. (Plur. خط وط). خط استوا ḵẖat̤t̤-i-istiwā or خط اعتدال ḵẖat̤t̤-i-iﻌtidāl, The equator. خط آنا يا نکلنا ḵẖat̤t̤-ānā or -nikalnā, To appear (the beard). خط بنانا يا بنوانا ḵẖat̤t̤-banānā or -banwānā, To shave the beard. خط جدي ḵẖat̤t̤-i-jadī, The tropick of Capricorn. خط سرطان ḵẖat̤t̤-i-sarat̤ān, The tropick of Cancer. خط شعاعي ḵẖat̤t̤-i-shuʼāﻌ-īﻌ, A ray of the sun; also, the name of a kind of writing. خط عمود ḵẖat̤t̤-i-aﻌmūd, A perpendicular line. خط کتابت ḵẖat̤t̤-i-kitābat, Correspondence. خط گلذار ḵẖat̤t̤-i-gulẕār, A kind of flourished hand writing. خط متوازي ḵẖat̤t̤-i-mutawāzī, A parallel line. خط مستدير ḵẖat̤t̤-ī-mustadīr, A circular line. خط مستقيم ḵẖat̤t̤-i-mustaḳīm, A right line. خط منحني ḵẖat̤t̤-i-munḥanī, A curved…
subs: s.m. 'Sun-shaped characters,' a form of Persian writing in which the curved tails of the letters are portions of a circle
v.n. A letter to arrive; — down to appear (on the face), beard, &c. to begin to sprout
or ḵẖat̤t̤-ě-ʻětidāl, s.m. The equinoctial line, the equator
v.n. Handwriting to fall off or become worse
To shave (the chin, &c.), or to have (the chin) shaved; to dress or trim the beard
s.m. 'Egg-shaped characters,' a form of Persian writing in which the curved tails of the letters are segments of an oval
adj. Marked with lines or streaks; striped (as cloth, &c.)
v.n. Handwriting to improve or to become good
ḵẖat̤t̤-ě-shǒʻāʼī, s.m. Rays of the sun; a solar ray; — a kind of writing
s.m. The smallest Arabic or Persian handwriting
s.m. A kind of flourished handwriting
s.m. An unclaimed letter; — a release or acquittance in writing: a writing relinquishing all claim (to)

To have the beard shaved off
To copy the handwriting (of); to forge a handwriting

s.m. A kind of Arabic writing (see nasḵẖ)

def: s.m. A pit (for storing grain, or ice, &c.), a subterraneous granary ( = khāt, and khātā); — store, heap, stock.
more: n. m. khattī , n. f. S. कृदर 1. A store; heap; stock. 2. (Bhoj. khād ) An underground granary. 3. An ice pit. 4. ( khuttī ) Treasure; wealth. Mere pās kyā khuttī gaṛī hai? khattī bharnā , v. n. To heap or store up grain in a khattī. khattī kā nāj , n. m. Bad grain.

adj. S. शट Sour (ترش). Saīyāṅ jī ne is dunyā meṅ lakhoṅ rupaiyā baṭṭe, Kabhūṅ na lāë laḍḍū ham ko, ber khīlāë khaṭṭe . The wife's complaint. My husband in this world has given lacs away, For me not e'en a sugar plum, a sour plum nay. khaṭṭā , n. m. A citron. khaṭṭā chūk , adj. Very sour. khaṭṭā khānā , v. a. 1. To taste the bitters; reap the tares. Mujh se bole to khaṭṭā khāoge . 2. To bear malice. Tum ne kis bāt pe khaṭṭā khā rakkhā hai? khaṭ'ṭā mīṭhā h. , v. n. To water (one's mouth). Jab nazar āī mujhe shokh ke sīne kī bahār Khaṭṭā mīṭhā sā lagā hone merā dil ek bār . Nazīr. When I her bosom's gorgeous beauty saw My longing from my mouth did water draw. 2. To have a misunderstanding or quarrel. khaṭṭā mīṭhā, khaṭ miṭhṛā , adj. Sweet and sour. khaṭṭā h. , v. n. To be or turn sour ( turshānā ). jī khaṭṭā h. , v. n. To be soured, offended, displeased. Un se merā jī khaṭṭā ho gayā .
s. m. A cavity in which grain is kept. See کهات sign. 2.

def: s.m. (dialec.) A preparation of opium, alum, turmeric, lemon-juice, &c. levigated together in a copper vessel and heated (it is applied to the eyes in ophthalmia, to the head in headaches, and to sprains, &c.).
adj. (f. -ī), Acid, sour, tart, harsh; — s.m. (local) A citron
more: adj. Acid, sour. کهٿا سا khaṭṭā-sā, adj. Sourish, acetous, acescent. کهٿا ساگ khaṭṭā-sāg, s. m. The herb sorrel.
subs: (-kā), To taste the bitters (of); to reap the bitter fruit (of); — khaṭṭā khānā or khā-rakhnā (-se), To feel bitterness or aversion (to); to bear malice (against a person, on account of, -par or -pe)
or khaṭ-mīṭhā or miṭhṛā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Sour and sweet; — a mixed taste of sour and sweet; an agreeable taste; — khaṭ-mīṭhā, s.m. A kind of fruit (which has a sour-sweet taste)
To eat acid and sweet things; to take the bitter with the sweet, to take things as they go
To have an agreeable taste or sensation; to feel a longing (for); to water (with longing, the mouth); — to be displeased; to have a misunderstanding or quarrel
v.n. To be or become sour, to turn sour; — to be displeased or offended (with, -se)
ex: The lemon is sour to the taste.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi کھٹّا (khaṭṭā, “yellow, sour”), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀔𑀝𑁆𑀝 (khaṭṭa), from Sanskrit खट्ट (khaṭṭa). The expected outcome from direct inheritance is کھاٹا (khāṭā), so the lack of vowel lengthening before a geminate consonant means that the word was probably borrowed from Punjabi.

ety: khaṭṭā, q.v.+pan = प्पणं = S. aff. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Sourness, acidity; — moroseness, severity, harshness, sternness.

ety: khaṭṭā, q.v.+pan = प्पणं = S. aff. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Sourness, acidity; — moroseness, severity, harshness, sternness.
s.m. The civet or zebet cat, Viverra zibetha; a pole-cat; a wild cat.
adj. superl. (from خطّ ḵẖat̤t̤ ) used substantively, A good writer, a penman.
s.m. A practiser of the art of writing, a good writer, a caligraphist.
s.f. A pit or cavity (for grain, &c.), a subterraneous granary, &c. ( = khattā)
subs: 'To fill a grain-pit,' to store up (grain) in a pit.

def: adj. Linear, lineal; — in writing; literal; — of or pertaining to the beard, &c.; — of, or belonging to, or made at, Ḵẖat̤ (a place in Arabia, famous for the spears made there); — s.f. A spear made at ḵẖat̤.
more: adj. 1. Linear, in writing, of the beard, &c. 2. or ḵẖit̤t̤ī, Of ḵẖat̤t̤ or ḵẖit̤t̤ (spears, so named from the place where they were bought).

def: s.m. A hunter, one who lives by killing and selling game (see khaṭīk, the popular form).
more: s. m. A hunter, one who lives by killing and selling game.

def: s.m. The second (the military and regal) caste of Hindūs; a member of that caste; (locally) a grain-merchant, or grocer, a baniyā.
more: n. m. khatrānī , n. f. S. क्षत्री A Panjābī Banya or grocer. Khattarī putram, kabhī na mitram, jab dekho jab dagam dagā . Prov. A Khatri's son is no one's friend, deceit and guile his only end.
s. m. One of the four Hindū casts, a man of the military tribe.

def: s.m. A labourer, hireling.
s.f. A noble woman, a lady, matron (a common termination of Mohammadan female names, e.g. Jamīla Ḵẖātūn)
more: Mah. n. f. 1. The lady of the house ; matron; madam. 2. A common termination of Mah. female names, as Jamīlā Khātūn. /?/khātūn-i-jannat , Mah. n. f. lit. the queen of heaven. The title of Fatima the daughter of Muhammad.
s. f. A lady, a matron. خاتون جنت ḵẖātūn-i-jannat, Queen of heaven (a title given to Fātima daughter of Muhammad).
subs: Queen of Paradise (a title given to Fātima, daughter of Mohammad).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَاتُون (xātūn).

def: oblique plural of کھاتا (khātā, “account”)
s.f. A bedstead, couch, cot; a swing; a hammock
subs: (˚vā+an˚), s.m. A club shaped like the foot of a bedstead, or a club with a skull at the top, considered as the weapon of Śiva (and carried by ascetics and Jogīs); one of the attributes of Śiva.

def: s.f. 'Bed-sickness'; taking to bed in the sulks or dumps; feigning sickness
subs: To take to bed in the dumps, to feign sickness.

def: s.m. A dunghill, dust-heap.
s.m. = khatwārā, q.v. s.m. A dunghill, dust-heap.
s.f. 'Bed-sickness'; taking to bed in the sulks or dumps; feigning sickness
subs: To take to bed in the dumps, to feign sickness.

def: s.f. 'Bed-sickness'; taking to bed in the sulks or dumps; feigning sickness
subs: To take to bed in the dumps, to feign sickness.

ety: khaṭā or khaṭṭā, q.v.+S. आलः, with euphonic y
def: adj. (dialec.) Sour, cross, crabbed, morose, surly, gruff, harsh.
s.m. corr. of qat̤-zan, q.v.s.v. qat̤. adj. Sufficient, enough (see faqat̤).
s.m.f. Eating; that eats; voracious, gluttonous; (met.) that takes, or receives; that sustains or undergoes, &c.; — a great eater, a glutton; one who takes or receives (bribes)
subs: adj. & s.m.f. Extravagant, prodigal; — an extravagant person, &c.

def: s.f. A cave, cavern, den (of a wild beast), chasm (in a mountain); a pit, hole (in the ground); a subterranean vault or storehouse (for grain, &c.).
more: s. m. (dakh.) A subterranean storehouse or vault for grain.

def: s.m. corr. of khoṅdhā, q.v. s.m. A (bird's) nest.

ety: inf. n. of خاف, for خوف 'to fear'
def: s.m. Fear, dread, terror
more: n. m. Fear (H. بهے). khauf dikhā-kar bāz rakhnā . To deter. khauf-nāk , adj. Frightful (بهيانک). jald marne kā khauf rakhnā , To fear the approach of death. fauran jān se halāk karne kā khauf denā . To threaten instant death. kisī shakhs ko khauf meṅ ḍālnā . To intimidate; threaten; alarm.

s. m. Fear. خوف رجا ḵẖauf rajā, s. m. (Fear and hope) Suspense. خوف کرنا ḵẖauf karnā, To fear. خوف ناک ḵẖauf-nāk, adj. 1. Frightful, terrifying. 2. Frightened, afraid, terrified. خوفناکي ḵẖauf-nāk-ī, s. f. Terrour, panick, fright, alarm.
subs: ḵẖauf dikhānā, v.t. To impress (one) with fear (of, -kā), to frighten, terrify; to threaten
To evince fear (in, -meṅ); to be afraid (of, -se), to fear
(dil-meṅ), To give place to fear (in the heart)
v.t. To put into a state of fear, to frighten, to intimidate
adj. Frightful, terrifying; — afraid, frightened, terrified
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَوف (xawf), from Arabic خَوْف (ḵawf).

def: sad, sadness
wety: From Pashto خفگان (xaufgān).

def: horrible, terrifying, frightful, dreadful
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خوفناک (xawfnāk).

def: afraid, frightened, terrified
s. m. (dakh.) A porcupine.
s.m. (dialec.) Scale of a fish, &c.); quill (of a porcupine)
more: n. S. खल्ल or शल्क Scales.

def: s. m. (dakh.) A porcupine.
s.m. (dialec.) Scale of a fish, &c.); quill (of a porcupine)
s.f. The act of boiling.
v.t. To cause to boil; to boil (water, &c.), to make boiling hot.
more: v. a. Caus. of کهولنا q. v.

def: s.f. (Dakkhinī) The sole (fish), Pleuronectes solea.

ety: khaul˚ = S. क्ष्वेड(ति), rt. क्ष्विड्; or S. क्ष्वेल(ति), rt. क्ष्वेल्
def: v.n. To boil, to be agitated by heat; — to burn (mentally).
more: v. n. 1. To boil; overboil. 2. To burn mentally. Andar hī andar khaultī hūṅ . Wom. khaul'tā , adj. Boiling hot. Khaultā pānī. khaul'an , n. f. Boiling.
v. n. To boil, to be agitated by heat.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Boiling; boiling hot.
adj. (dakh. i. q. حرصي) Greedy (?).
Bhoj. The sound of coughing. khauṅ khauṅ k . W.; khoṅkhī ānā, khokhnā , v. a. To cough ( khāṅsnā ).

s.f. = anjalī, q.v. s.f. = anjlā, q.v.; — as much of anything as can be contained in the hands placed side by side with the palms hollowed; a mode of salutation, or of supplication, by carrying the hands placed side by side (as described under anjlā) to the forehead.
s.f. Coughing; sound of coughing
s.m. An old slipper or shoe.

W. n. m. An old shoe, also called pātan . ☞ In the Holī festival a necklace of these is hung from the neck of a banyah who is led on an ass as a bridegroom to the Chauk or to a cross road. Jo tauṅh levan āve manne sīs khauṅs bajvāuṅgī, Jo tauṅh hūkā chhoḍe nāhīṅ maiṅ pīhar uṭh jāuṅgī . Rus. Wom. If you to take me come my slippers on your head I'll sound, Give up your pipe, I say, or for my father's house I'm bound.
s.f. The tilak or (crescent-shaped) mark made on the forehead (of Hindūs, worshippers of Śiva) with sandal-wood, saffron, &c.
more: s. f. The marks which Hindūs make on their foreheads with sandal wood, saffron, &c.

def: s.m. The rot (among sheep); the falling off of the hairs.
more: (corrupt. of خورا) s. m. The rot (among sheep), the falling off of the hairs.

def: s.m. Scraping or pawing the ground with the hoof (as an ox); pawing the ground and bellowing
more: n. m. H. khur hoof. 1. Digging the earth with the hoofs. 2. The bellowing of a bull. 3. Quarrel ( ṭanṭā ). Mujh se khaurū na lānā! khaurū lānā , v. 1. To paw and bellow. 2. To defy.
subs: To scrape or paw the ground with the hoof
(-se), To commence pawing the ground and bellowing; to bellow defiantly (at).

ety: inf. n. of خوض 'to wade; to enter'
def: s.m. Diving (into), entering; considering; consulting; purposing, resolving, intending; consideration, attention; deep thought; research
more: n. m. 1. Deep thought or meditation ( gahrā khayāl ). 2. Search; research. khauz k. , Ped. v. a. To be in deep thought or contemplation.
s. m. Purposing, resolving, intending, considering, consulting, attention.
subs: (-meṅ), To dive (into), enter (into), go deep (into), to consider deeply or with attention; to be lost in thought or meditation.

def: s.f. Manure, &c. ( = khāt or khad, qq.v.). s.f. Rubbish, refuse, filth, dirt, dung, muck, manure; anything rotten or decayed
more: s. f. Manure: see کهاد khād. ¶ khāʼū (from کهانا khānā ) Glutton, (in compos.) eating, eater. ¶ khāʼo, in compos. s. (dakh.) Eating.
subs: or khāt ḍālnā (-meṅ), To dung, to manure (land)

def: s.m. The shoulder; shoulder-blade.
more: n. m. The shoulder. Khave se khavā chhiltā thā . The crowd was so dense that they rubbed shoulder to shoulder.

def: s.m. An eater ( = khāne-wālā).

v.t. 'To cause to eat,' to feed, &c. ( = khilānā, q.v.). v.t. To cause to eat; to give food to, to feed; to entertain; to cause to take (as medicine, or the air, or an oath, &c.); to give, to administer (poison, &c.); to cause to suffer or undergo (as a beating, &c.)
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Who feeds; — feeder, supporter, sustainer
s.f. A woman who supports her lover.

def: s.m. Lord, master; husband
more: n. m. See patī. Āp mere mālik aur khāvind haiṅ ! You are my lord and master! khāvind salāmat' , My lord! khāvind k . v. a. To get a husband; to marry.
(proper. ḵẖāwand, contract. of خد اوند) s. m. Lord, master, husband.
subs: To take a husband, to marry.
wety: From Classical Persian خاوند (xāwand).

def: s.m. pl. (of P. ḵẖān), Lords, nobles, &c.
more: s. plur. of خان ḵẖān q. v.

def: s.m. The west; (but often used by poets for) the east; — the sun
more: s. m. 1. The west. 2. The sun. 3. The east.
subs: s.m. 'The monarch of the east,' the sun.

def: s.m. pl. (of ḵẖāwar), The east and west; — name of a district in Ḵẖurāsān.
more: s. A country in Ḵẖurāsān: the east and west.

def: adj. Of or belonging to Ḵẖāwarān, q.v.
more: adj. Of the country of Ḵẖāwarān.

def: adj. Western; eastern; — solar.
s.m. pl. (of ḵẖārijī), Heretics, schismatics.
more: s. plur. of خارجي ḵẖārijī, q. v.

def: s.m. Events infringing the usual course of nature, unusual things, miracles.
more: s. plur. of (خرق or خارق) Contrary to nature, unusual things, miracles.

ety: the arabicized form of P. ḵẖawarna; Old P. ḵẖvarna, fr. Z. hvare = S. svar
def: s.m. A magnificent edifice, a palace; the lofty palace built by Nǒʻmān-bin-Munẕir for king Bahrām Gor, in Babylonia.
more: n. prop. A very lofty palace, which Nuﻌmān son of Munẕir built for Bahrām Gor, king of Persia.

def: s.f. The office or duty of an attendant; — the place where one sits behind a great man upon an elephant; the back seat in a hauda, &c
subs: v.n. To sit in attendance behind a great man on an elephant.

ety: of ḵẖāṣṣ, 'peculiar,' &c. and of ḵẖāṣṣa, 'distinguished person'
def: s.f. Bidding for the seat of honour (in the car which bears the Jain god; cf. ḵẖawāṣī)
more: n. pl. of خاص 1. Personal attendants, usually the favorite or confidential attendant of a person of rank. 1. Khavāsoṅ kā aur lauṅḍiyoṅ kā hujūm, Mahal kī woh chohleṅ, woh āpas kī dhūm . Mīr. Hasan. 2. Jaisā ūṅṭ lambā, taisā gadhā khavās! Prov. A tall camel, a donkey for a servant. (Like master like man). 2. A favourite; a companion. 3. Nature ( khāsyat ). khavās-bolī , n. f. Bidding for the chief seat in the car which bears the Jain god. khavās o avām , Nobles and plebeians ( khās o ām ).

s. plur. (of خاصّ or خاصّه) 1. Attendants, domesticks. 2. Grandees, ministers of state, people of quality or distinct from the vulgar, gentry. 3. Properties, qualities, attributes. 4. A page or a female attendant on the great. 5. A favourite. خواص وعوام ḵẖawāṣṣ o aﻌwāmm, Nobles and plebeians.
subs: s.m. The apartments or habitation allotted to the domestics (of a great man); the servants' quarters
s.m. pl. Nobles and plebeians, high and low ( = ḵẖaṣṣ-o-ʻām).

def: s.m. pl. (of خاصة 'a property'), Properties, qualities, attributes
s.f. = khāṭ, q.v. s.f. Rubbish, refuse, filth, dirt, dung, muck, manure; anything rotten or decayed
subs: or khāt ḍālnā (-meṅ), To dung, to manure (land)

def: s.f. pl. (of ḵẖātūn, q.v.), Ladies.
wety: Independently borrowed from Arabic خَوَاتِين (ḵawātīn).

def: ladies and gentlemen
s.m. (f.?), A green field, a sown field, a field of green wheat or barley; green wheat or barley cut for fodder ( = vulg. ḵẖūd).
more: n. f. Unripe wheat or barley crop used as fodder.
s. m. A green field, a sown field, green grass cut for cattle, green corn.

def: adv. In the manner of a lord or master, like a master, lordly.
s.f. Dominion, mastership; protection, patronage; favour, kindness.
more: n. f. Kindness; favor; patronage; protection. 1. Ain khāvindī hai . 2. Āp kī khāvindī hai .

def: s.m. An egg; a testicle
more: n. m. 1. Membrum virile. Ūṅchā makān jiskā hai pach-khanā so āyā, Ūpar kā khan ṭapak-kar jab pānī nīche āyā, Us ne to apne ghar meṅ hai shor o gul machāyā, Muflis pukārte haiṅ jāne hamārā khāyā! Nazīr. 2. Testicles. Sir se khāyā bhārī . Prov. His testicles are heavier than his head. (A big hat on a small head.). khāyā bāshad h. , ( Slang ). v. n. To go to pot. khāyā-bardār , ( Slang ). n. m. A lickspittle; a cringing, obsequious fellow. khāyā bardārī k., khāyā sahlānā , ( Slang ) v. a. To cringe or fawn; to beslaver.

s. m. 1. A testicle. 2. An egg. خايه بردار ḵẖāya-bardār, A cringing fellow, fawner. خايه برداري کرنا ḵẖāya-bardār-ī karnā, To cringe, to fawn. خايه چمانا ḵẖāya chumānā, To disregard, to be disobedient, to be stubborn, not to submit to obedience. خايه سـﮩ‍لانا ḵẖāya sahlānā, To curry favour. خايه کشيده ḵẖāya-kashīda, s. m. An eunuch.
subs: s.m. A cringing or obsequious fellow, a fawner
v.t. To disregard, treat with contempt; to be disobedient, be stubborn, not to submit to obedience
(-kā), To curry favour (with); to cringe, &c. ( = ḵẖāya-bardārī karnā)

ety: v.n. fr. خال, for خيل 'to think, to fancy,' &c.
def: s.m. Thought, opinion, surmise, suspicion, conception, idea, notion, fancy, imagination, conceit. whim, chimera; consideration; regard, deference; apprehension; care, concern; — an imaginary form, apparition, vision, spectre, phantom, shadow, delusion; — a kind of song
more: n. m. خيل he thought. See bichār . 1. Opinion; impression; notion. 2. Whim; conceit; craze; fancy. 3. Apprehension ( bhaë , 2). Piyāre! hamāre milne se lāo na kuchh khayāl! Nazīr. 4. Care; concern. 5. Deference; respect; regard; consideration. 6. A kind of song. khayāl-i-bātil , Misconception; foolish imagination; a wrong idea; a false notion. khayāl bāṅdhnā , v. a. 1. To form a conception; to imagine; to represent a thought or image in the mind. 2. To arrange one's thoughts. 3. To build castles in the air. khayāl-bandī , n. f. lit . weaving a chain of thoughts. Castle-building; a picture of the imagination; fiction. khayāl paṛnā , v. n. 1. To occur to or strike one. 2. To give one's mind to. khayāl-i-khām . A crude notion; a vain or ridiculous idea. khayāl rakhnā , v. n. To bear in mind. khayāl se bāhar . Inconceivable. khayāl-i-fāsid , n. m. A false notion; an idle fancy. 2. Loose or w…

improp. ḵẖiyāl (from خال) s. m. 1. Imagination, fancy, thought, idea, consideration, apprehension, conceit, suspicion, opinion, notion. 2. Phantom, delusion, vision. 3. A kind of song. خيال باطل ḵẖayāl-i-bāt̤il, Foolish imagination. خيال پرست ḵẖayāl-parast, adj. Fanciful, visionary, castle-builder. خيال پڙنا ḵẖayāl paṛnā, To pursue an object. خيال پلاوپکانا ḵẖayāl pulāʼo pakānā, To indulge in vain speculations or absurd fancies, to build castles in the air. خيال چهوڙنا ḵẖayāl chhoṛnā, To be out of conceit with. خيال خلافت ḵẖayāl-i-ḵẖilāfat, Ambition, خيال خام ḵẖayāl-i-ḵẖām, A vain, ridiculous idea. خيال کرنا ḵẖayāl karnā, To imagine, to fancy, to think.
subs: s.m. Foolish imagination; a wrong idea, false notion, misconception
(-kā), To form a conception or idea (of), to imagine; to frame a thought or image in the mind; to build castles in the air; — to arrange (one's) thoughts
s.f. Structure of the imagination; — imagining, dreaming, castle-building
adj. & s.m. Fanciful; — a visionary; a castle-builder
v.n. To occur (to, -ko), to seem; — to give the mind (to, -par), fix the thoughts (on), to pursue (an object)
see ḵẖayālī pulāʼo pakānā, s.v. ḵẖayālī
(-kā), To relinquish all thought (of); to be out of conceit (with)
s.m. A crude notion; a vain or ridiculous idea
v.t. To bear in mind; — ḵẖayāl-se bāhar, adj. Inconceivable
s.m. A false notion, vicious idea; idle fancy; — corrupt or lewd thoughts
(-ko, or kā), To think (of), to consider, imagine, fancy, conceive; to regard attentively, to attend (to), direct the attention (to), to mind, observe; to form designs (against, -kā)
v.n. To be constantly, or always, thinking (of a thing)
(-ko), To give no thought to, pay no regard to, not to take into consideration, to disregard; to consider as unworthy of thought; to show no consideration or respect for
(with dat., -ko, of pers., and gen., -kā, of thing), To lose all recollection (of), to forget (about), to escape (one's) memory
v.t., 1˚ = ḵẖayāl-meṅ na lānā, q.v.; — 2˚, to think nothing of, to pass (offensive speech, &c.) unnoticed, not to take amiss
adv. In consideration (of, -ke), with regard (to), with reference (to); out of consideration or regard (for).
ex: یہ سَب کُچھ تُمہارے خَیالوں میں ہَے!
ye sab kuch tumhāre xayālõ mẽ hai!
This is all in your imagination!
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَیال, borrowed from Arabic خَيال (ḵayāl).

def: s.m. Apparition, spectre, phantom, &c. ( = ḵẖayāl, q.v.). s.m. Thought, opinion, surmise, suspicion, conception, idea, notion, fancy, imagination, conceit. whim, chimera; consideration; regard, deference; apprehension; care, concern; — an imaginary form, apparition, vision, spectre, phantom, shadow, delusion; — a kind of song
subs: s.m. Foolish imagination; a wrong idea, false notion, misconception
(-kā), To form a conception or idea (of), to imagine; to frame a thought or image in the mind; to build castles in the air; — to arrange (one's) thoughts
s.f. Structure of the imagination; — imagining, dreaming, castle-building
adj. & s.m. Fanciful; — a visionary; a castle-builder
v.n. To occur (to, -ko), to seem; — to give the mind (to, -par), fix the thoughts (on), to pursue (an object)
see ḵẖayālī pulāʼo pakānā, s.v. ḵẖayālī
(-kā), To relinquish all thought (of); to be out of conceit (with)
s.m. A crude notion; a vain or ridiculous idea
v.t. To bear in mind; — ḵẖayāl-se bāhar, adj. Inconceivable
s.m. A false notion, vicious idea; idle fancy; — corrupt or lewd thoughts
(-ko, or kā), To think (of), to consider, imagine, fancy, conceive; to regard attentively, to attend (to), direct the attention (to), to mind, observe; to form designs (against, -kā)
v.n. To be constantly, or always, thinking (of a thing)
(-ko), To give no thought to, pay no regard to, not to take into consideration, to disregard; to consider as unworthy of thought; to show no consideration or respect for
(with dat., -ko, of pers., and gen., -kā, of thing), To lose all recollection (of), to forget (about), to escape (one's) memory
v.t., 1˚ = ḵẖayāl-meṅ na lānā, q.v.; — 2˚, to think nothing of, to pass (offensive speech, &c.) unnoticed, not to take amiss
adv. In consideration (of, -ke), with regard (to), with reference (to); out of consideration or regard (for).

def: s.m. Vain thoughts, absurd ideas, ill-grounded expectations.
more: n. m. pl. of خيال Imaginations; fancies; thoughts; ideas. khayālāt-i-lā-tāil . Vain thoughts; absurd ideas.
s. m. plur. (of خيال) Imaginations, fancies. خيالات لا طايل ḵẖayālāt-i-lā-t̤āʼịl, Ill-grounded expectation.

def: adj. Ideal, imaginary; fanciful, capricious; fantastical; visionary; chimerical
more: adj. 1. Imaginary; ideal. 2. Fanciful; fantastical; visionary; Utopain. khayālī pulāo pakānā , v. a. To indulge in vain speculation or absurd fancies; to build castles in the air.
adj. Fanciful, capricious, fantastical, visionary, imaginary, ideal.
subs: To indulge in vain speculations or absurd fancies; to build castles in the air.

def: oblique plural of خَیَال (xayāl)
s.f. A kind of violet.
more: s. f. A kind of violet.

def: s.m.(?) Pottery, earthen vessel or vessels; potsherd; broken shells.
more: s. An earthen vessel, potsherds, broken shells.

def: s.m.(?) = ḵẖazaf, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of ḵẖizāna), Repositories; treasuries, &c.
s.m. A treasurer, cash-keeper
more: n. m. A treasurer; cashier; cash-keeper. khazānchī-garī , n. f. The office of a treasurer.
s. m. A treasurer. خزانچي گري ḵẖazānchī-garī, s. f. Treasurership.

ety: act. part. n. of خزن 'to store,' &c.
def: s.m. A treasurer, keeper or guardian (of property), custodian.
more: (part. act. of خزن) s. m. Treasurer.

def: s.m. A treasury, &c. ( = ḵẖazāna, q.v.).
s.f. A coarse kind of silk (cf. P. qaz).
more: s. f. A coarse kind of silk-cloth.

def: n. f. 1. Dust; ashes. 2. Night-soil ( bhisṭā ). khe khānā , v. n. See jhak mārnā , 2.

r: ḵẖe, ḵẖāʼě-manqūt̤a, ḵẖā-ě-mǒʻjama, ch, loch, abjad
def: the tenth letter of the Hindūstānī alphabet (the seventh of the Arabic, from which it is borrowed), called ḵẖāʼě-manqūt̤a, and ḵẖā-ě-mǒʻjama. It is one of the faucial or guttural letters, and has no corresponding sound in English, but is approximately expressed by the Scottish pronunciation of ch in the word loch. As a numeral (see abjad) it denotes 600; — in astronomy it denotes the planet Mars.
more: guttural, also called khāë manqūtā or muٴajma . It has no corresponding sound in the English language, but is expressed by the Scottish pronounciation of ch in the word 'loch' or Ger. ich . In abjad , q. v. it stands for 600, and in Ast. for the planet Mars.

r: khe-ćar, khai-ćar, khe, kha, ćara, lit.
ety: khe, loc. of kha+ćara, lit. 'moving in the sky'
def: s.m. (f.-ī), A bird; a planet; a vidyādhar, or kind of demi-god; genii.
more: s. m. 1. A planet. 2. A bird. 3. Genii.

def: corr. form of khiṅćnā, q.v. (and cf. kheṅćnā).
s.m. Lassitude, depression, despondency; faintness; fatigue; pain; regret, sorrow, grief, affliction, trouble, distress; — complaint, disorder, ailment
more: n. f. S. खिद, , Pālī, khedo to suffer pain. Sickness; disorder ( bīmārī ).
subs: (-ko), To give pain (to), to grieve, distress, &c.
(-kā), To grieve (about), to bewail, regret; — to be distressing or injurious (to); to be disagreeable or obnoxious (to)

def: s.m. The trap or enclosure in which elephants are caught; — a strong bird (or other animal) which beats and drives away other birds, &c.
more: n. m. 1. The trap or enclosure in which elephants are caught (اوگن 3). 2. A strong bird, esp. a bulbul which beats and drives away other birds.
s. m. The trap or enclosure in which elephants are caught.

def: adj. Of or relating to a khedā or elephant-trap
subs: s.m. Expenses incurred in catching wild elephants; a cess levied for that purpose.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Grieving, sorrowing, regretting, regretful, repentant ( = khedit, q.v.).
part. & s.m. (f. -ā), Disturbed, annoyed, harassed; distressed, afflicted, pained, grieved; sorrowful, repentant; — a distressed person; a sorrowful person, &c.

ety: khed˚ = S. खिन्ट्(ति), rt. खिद्; or = S. खेद
def: v.n. = khed karnā, q.v.s.v. khed. s.m. Lassitude, depression, despondency; faintness; fatigue; pain; regret, sorrow, grief, affliction, trouble, distress; — complaint, disorder, ailment
more: v. n. 1. To be disagreeable, obnoxious. 2. To be hurtful, or injurious.
v. a. To run after, persecute, pursue, to chase.
subs: (-ko), To give pain (to), to grieve, distress, &c.
(-kā), To grieve (about), to bewail, regret; — to be distressing or injurious (to); to be disagreeable or obnoxious (to)

ety: khed˚ = S. खेदय(ति), caus. of rt. खिद्
def: v.t. To run after, to persecute; to hunt, chase, chevy, drive away.
v. a. (dakh.) See کهيدنا khednā.
s.f. Dry brambles; — a hedge of brambles or thorns.
more: n. f. 1. Dry brambles. 2. A hedge.

def: s.m. = kekṛā or keṅkṛā, q.v.
s.f. A thin crisp cake.
more: n. f. Hin. A thin crisp cake.
